summary.intent: Address Codex code review findings on PR #694 (ars-ui) for RatingGroup and TagGroup components, push fixes, and drive the PR toward approval through iterative Codex review cycles.
summary.outcome: Three rounds of Codex review were completed. Eight threads from round two and four threads from round three were fixed, replied to, and resolved. A third fix commit (addressing slider aria-valuetext count context, controlled TagGroup selection request/effect payloads, removed-tag persistence across SyncProps, and disabled-key filtering) passed all local gates (fmt, clippy, conformance, proptest, xci-fast) and was pushed. CI had a transient rust-lld bus error on ars-i18n that was resolved by rerunning failed jobs.

repo: The project uses `cargo xci-fast` as a local fast-CI gate covering fmt, workspace check, clippy, and nextest unit tests — all 10 steps must pass before committing.
Codex review is triggered by posting `@codex review` as a PR comment; progress is tracked via GitHub reactions (👀 = in progress, 👍 = approved) on that comment.
Review threads are resolved via GitHub GraphQL `resolveReviewThread` mutation after posting a reply referencing the fix commit SHA.
The project uses `cargo insta` for snapshot testing; intentional output changes require `cargo insta accept` before tests pass.
no-default-features build failures exist in pre-existing unrelated modules — failures not in the touched files are considered acceptable to proceed.
Controlled bindables have `sync_controlled` / `is_controlled` / `get` / `set` API in ars-core; TagGroup and RatingGroup must emit SyncProps effects for all prop-driven attribute changes.
TagGroup selection initialization and prop sync must normalize by selection mode (clear for None, single-key for Single) and filter out disabled keys.
RatingGroup needs to record latest user-requested value, expose `Api::requested_value`, and emit a typed `ValueChange` effect for both controlled and uncontrolled rating requests.
Slider aria-valuetext for RatingGroup should include count context (e.g., '3 of 5 stars'), requiring `f64::from(count)` for a lossless cast rather than `as`.

code.finding: aria-valuetext generation and ValueChange effect emission; requested_value API surface added in this region
count field type is u32; must use f64::from(count) not `as f64` to avoid clippy lint on potentially lossy cast
RemoveTag, SyncProps, and selection initialization logic; removed keys must survive prop sync and selection must filter disabled keys
Controlled selection request/effect payload surface; SelectionChange effect must carry requested-selection data

workflow: Iterative Codex review requires polling both comment reactions and PR review threads after each `@codex review` trigger — the reaction lifecycle and thread list are separate signals.
Transient CI failures (linker bus errors) should be resolved by rerunning failed jobs rather than modifying source code.
Focused test targets (`cargo test -p ars-components --lib data_display::rating_group::tests`) should be run before broadening to full conformance/proptest suites to surface type mismatches early.
Snapshot test failures from intentional output changes must be accepted with `cargo insta accept` before rerunning — they are not real test failures.
Clippy `-D warnings` must pass before committing; prefer `f64::from(x)` over `x as f64` for integer-to-float conversions to satisfy the lint.

friction: Codex did not post a thumbs-up after the third review pass, requiring manual thread inspection to discover new findings rather than getting a clear signal.
GitHub CI failed with a transient rust-lld bus error in ars-i18n unrelated to the PR's changes, requiring a job rerun and adding latency to the feedback loop.
The no-default-features build has pre-existing failures in unrelated modules that produce noise when checking the full workspace, making it hard to confirm the touched files are clean.
Type mismatch on the u32 count field (needed f64::from vs as cast) was caught only at test-time, not during the patch application step.
Multiple polling sleep cycles (60s–120s) were needed to wait for Codex and CI reactions, creating a slow iterative loop with no push-based notification.
open_items: Pre-existing no-default-features build failures in modules outside rating_group/tag_group were not fixed — these should be addressed separately.
Codex did not issue a final thumbs-up approval after the third review pass; a fourth `@codex review` cycle may be needed once CI is fully green on the new commit.
The ars-i18n rust-lld bus error is a flaky CI issue that should be investigated to determine if it is an infra problem or a reproducible failure.
The CI rerun result was not confirmed in the transcript — the final green/red status of the rerun needs to be verified.
